_notes.file: Replaces the former 'image' and 'markdown' primitives. Use payload.mimeType to distinguish — e.g. 'image/jpeg', 'text/markdown', 'application/pdf'. Any IANA media type is valid.
_notes.node: A generic node type for use in user-defined trees. Items of type 'node' can appear under items of type 'tree' to form named additional tree structures beyond the built-in one.
_notes.tree: A named tree root. Kanecta has one built-in tree rooted at the all-zeros UUID. Additional trees are items of type 'tree' — their children are items of type 'node'. Useful for alternative navigation structures, tag hierarchies, org charts, etc.
_notes.component: A React component definition. body lives in meta.files.body = 'body.tsx'. Props use the parameterOrProp definition — same as function parameters.
_notes.formula: A reusable calculation — used wherever a computed value is needed: alias generation, primaryField derivation, derived fields on types, display labels. Three levels of increasing power: (1) template string — simple `{variable}` interpolation (e.g. `"TASK-{n}"`); (2) DSL — Excel-like expression evaluated by formulajs (MIT), with Kanecta-provided variables and extension functions such as ITEM(uuid, field) and SEQ(typeId); (3) functionId — UUID pointer to a `function` item for full programmatic power when the DSL is not sufficient. A formula item is a standalone item with its own UUID, allowing other items to reference it stably even if the expression changes. The consumer (e.g. a type's aliasFormulas field) holds the formulaId. Built-in template variables available at all levels: `n` (auto-incrementing integer scoped per type per workspace), `year`, `month`, `date` (current date parts), `type` (type name of the item being processed), `uuid8` (first 8 characters of the new item's UUID), `field.<name>` (the value of a named field on the item being processed).
_notes.function: A typed function definition. body lives in meta.files.body = 'body.ts' (filesystem/SQLite) or inline in payload.body (Postgres).
_notes.grant: An access control grant. Lives as a child of the item it controls, in the 'grants' aspect. Supports identity, namespace, and ReBAC principals.
_notes.relationship: A typed, first-class relationship between two items. typeId points to a relationship-type item; sourceId and targetId are the connected items. Coexists with inline [[uuid]] links — use relationship items when you need a typed, confidence-carrying, or historically tracked link.
_notes.relationship-type: Defines a relationship vocabulary entry — the meta-type for relationship instances, mirroring how type defines the vocabulary for object instances. Carries meta.directional (boolean) and meta.inverse (UUID of the paired inverse relationship-type item, e.g. depends-on ↔ enables). jsonSchema validates the data payload on relationship instances of this type. Nine canonical instances are bootstrapped as system items for the standard vocabulary (relates-to, depends-on, enables, contradicts, blocks, blocked-by, prerequisite-for, derived-from, supersedes). Custom relationship types are user-defined items of type relationship-type.
_notes.view: A specific perspective on an item: the item being viewed (itemId), the renderer (componentId), and the environment (contextId). The view's UUID is a single pointer to all three. Used for bookmarking, sharing, and bootstrapping (root.payload.entryPoint points to a view item).
_notes.context: An execution and display environment. Captures runtime (web, desktop, cli, mcp, pdf) and display position (tree-node, detail-panel, modal, preview, embed). capabilities[] is exploratory — records what the runtime supports (e.g. react, css, images). Expected to evolve through use.
_notes.type: A custom type definition. The meta-type — instances of every user-defined type (object items) live under their type item as their canonical home. payload is typePayload. type items themselves live under a bootstrapper-created collection tree under root.
_notes.alias: A human-readable string that resolves to a UUID. Distinct from symlink — a symlink is a tree-placement item (puts an item in another location in the tree); an alias is a string lookup (lets a user address an item by name). The alias string lives in item.value; lookup is case-insensitive. Aliases exist at three scopes: personal (visible only in the personal workspace), org (shared across the org workspace), and type (defined at the type level, usually formula-generated for all items of that type). When 2+ aliases match a lookup string the adapter surfaces the full list and the user chooses. Provisional aliases (provisional: true) are computed locally at creation time and confirmed as globally unique only at merge (PR pre-flight checks for alias conflicts). Lives under the aliased item (targetId = parentId) — this is its canonical home in the tree. The adapter maintains a derived aliases lookup table for O(1) resolution; that table is never the source of truth, it is rebuilt from items.
_notes.subscription: A live notification channel registered against a UUID. Someone or something wants to be notified when a specific item changes. Distinct from reference — a subscription is about change notification, not external dependency or deletion protection. Lives under the watched item in the "subscriptions" aspect. payload is subscriptionPayload.
_notes.reference: A declared dependency on a UUID from outside the item graph — physical (engraved, printed, QR code), external system (another database holds this UUID as a foreign key), function argument, function output, webhook, or reactive subscription. Unlike relationship, a reference asserts that the target UUID has reach beyond Kanecta itself. blockDeletion:true prevents deletion of the target until the reference is explicitly removed. Lives as a standalone item (like relationship) — targetId in payload, not constrained by parentId. The item_references derived index tracks structural references automatically; reference items cover declared/manual cases.
